articles.full_text:
Maharashtra Heatwave Alert: The State Faces Heatstroke Crisis! 297 Patients Recorded in Three Months; 8 Deaths, 18 Suspected Victims
This year, the rising temperatures in the state have resulted in a significant increase in heatstroke cases. Since March 1, a total of 297 heatstroke patients have been reported across the state, with 8 deaths. Additionally, 18 suspected deaths have also been recorded.
According to health department statistics, May saw the highest number of cases with 258 patients, while March recorded 7 and April 32 patients. The highest number of patients, 48, was found in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar district, followed by 28 in Nandurbar, 22 in Gadchiroli, and 21 in Nashik.
Due to heatstroke, two people each died in Akola, Jalgaon, and Amravati, while one person died in Gadchiroli and Latur. Notably, despite the intense heat, there have been no reports of heatstroke patients or deaths in Mumbai. Similarly, no incidents of heatstroke have been reported in Kolhapur, Dhule, Dharashiv, Sangli, and Sindhudurg districts.
Summary
- A total of 297 heatstroke patients have been reported in the state.
- So far, 8 deaths have been recorded.
- The highest number of patients was reported in May.
- Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar has the highest number of 48 patients.
- There are no cases in Mumbai and some districts.
- The health department has issued a warning for caution.
